page: Amazon Web Services r8in.48xlarge is an eighth-generation, memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 virtual central processing units (vCPUs) mapped to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture. It does not include local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. Network connectivity is a key feature of this configuration, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with the instance.

Given its high memory capacity and substantial network throughput, the r8in.48xlarge is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The lack of local storage means storage performance depends on network-attached volumes, representing a tradeoff for workloads requiring ultra-low latency local disk access. It is qualitatively positioned as a high-resource density instance suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, big data analytics, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and memory-bound containerized applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8in.48xlarge is an eighth-generation, memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz, it provides 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores and 1536.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive applications. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps but does not include local storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage volumes. This design represents a tradeoff, prioritizing massive memory capacity and high-speed network throughput over local disk performance. The r8in.48xlarge is engineered for demanding enterprise workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, memory-bound data analytics, and complex enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8in.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB RAM, a 3.9 GHz Intel processor, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, running on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel processors and high-speed network throughput for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by 3.9 GHz Intel processors.
1536.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
High-speed network performance with 300 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation.

Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise applications.
